prompts.title: Initial alert triage
prompts.question: I just started my shift. What's the current alert situation look like? I want the severity breakdown and what's the most urgent thing I need to look at first.

prompts.title: C2 beacon investigation
prompts.question: Suricata flagged Cobalt Strike beacon activity. Investigate the source host and the C2 server. What's the full picture?

prompts.title: Data exfiltration assessment
prompts.question: Alert says 'Possible Data Exfiltration - Large Outbound Transfer' from 10.10.5.22 to 198.51.100.77. How much data was transferred? Is this related to the C2 activity?

prompts.title: Internal scanning detection
prompts.question: I see SSH scan alerts from 10.10.3.15. What hosts were targeted and on what ports? Is this an internal threat?

prompts.title: SSH brute force investigation
prompts.question: Wazuh is showing repeated SSH login failures on web-server-01. Is this a brute force attack? Has anyone succeeded in logging in?

prompts.title: Sigma rule for C2 beacons
prompts.question: I need a Sigma rule to detect the Cobalt Strike beacon pattern we just identified — regular HTTPS connections to 203.0.113.45 on port 443. Make sure it's a valid, deployable rule.

prompts.title: Targeted port scan
prompts.question: We suspect the compromised host 10.10.5.22 is running a backdoor listener. Scan it for open ports — check the full range, not just the top 100. What services are exposed?

prompts.title: Full incident timeline
prompts.question: Build me a timeline of the entire incident involving 10.10.5.22. Pull from every relevant data source we have — network, endpoint, host — and correlate them to reconstruct the attack chain from initial compromise to data exfiltration.

prompts.title: Evidence inventory and integrity
prompts.question: IR team just dropped off forensic evidence. Can you catalog what we received, and get integrity hashes for suspicious_beacon.exe so we can submit for IOC matching?

prompts.title: Malware string analysis
prompts.question: We've got a suspicious executable called suspicious_beacon.exe in our forensic samples. Can you identify what kind of file it is and pull any readable indicators from it — I'm looking for C2 addresses, commands, or persistence mechanisms.
